subject: [PATCH v7 3/3] locking: Add contended_release tracepoint to sleepable locks

reviews.author: tip-bot2 Ilvokhin
reviews.summary: The reviewer noted that the contended_release tracepoint fires after the lock is released for some lock types, which may not be consistent across all lock types as mentioned in the patch description.

2026-06-12.patch_summary: This patch adds a new tracepoint called contended_release to the Linux kernel's locking mechanism. It fires when a lock is released under contention, allowing for correlation of lock hold time with waiter events by lock address. The patch modifies the slowpath unlock paths of various sleepable locks, including mutexes, rtmutexes, semaphores, and rwsems, to enable this tracepoint. The placement of the tracepoint varies depending on the lock type, but it generally fires before the lock is released or after the release but before the wake. This patch also adds support for reader/writer locks, firing the tracepoint for every reader releasing while a writer is waiting.
